steps.title: Use the trend and two anchor points
steps.content: The scatterplot has a strong positive trend, so choices C and D are out because their slopes are negative. To choose between the two positive-slope lines, use the approximate leftmost and rightmost data points from the graph: $(1,4)$ and $(8,26)$.

steps.title: Run the quick Desmos regression
steps.content: Enter the two anchor points as $x_1=[1,8]$ and $y_1=[4,26]$, then run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives a slope a little above $3$ and a y-intercept a little below $1$, so the line should look close to $y=3x+0.8$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B, $y=0.8x+3$, predicts only $9.4$ when $x=8$, far below the rightmost point near $26$. Choice A, $y=3x+0.8$, predicts $24.8$ when $x=8$ and $3.8$ when $x=1$, which stays close to both anchor points. Therefore A is correct.
